name: convert_html_to_pdf
description: Convert an HTML string or a publicly accessible URL into a PDF document. You can provide either raw HTML via the 'source' parameter or a URL. The API returns the PDF binary content directly (application/pdf) by default, or you can request a JSON response with a URL to the hosted PDF by setting 'filename'. This is the simplest conversion endpoint for basic use cases.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The HTML content to convert, or a publicly accessible URL (starting with http:// or https://). If it looks like a URL, PDFShift will fetch and render that page.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: If provided, the PDF will be stored on PDFShift's servers and a JSON response with the URL will be returned instead of raw binary PDF data.

parameters.name: sandbox
parameters.description: Set to true to use the sandbox mode which does not count against your credits. Sandbox PDFs have a watermark.
